title: Graph API preflight check times out in container (60s)
description: GraphClient.get_users(top=1) works locally (0.4s) but times out in the Azure App Service container after 60s. Likely causes: 1) ClientSecretCredential in container trying managed identity first before client_secret fallback, 2) Container cold start DNS resolution, 3) azure-identity SDK version mismatch. This cascades into 6 other MFA-related preflight failures.
close_reason: Fixed with lightweight /organization endpoint approach. Token acquisition now async-safe, 10s timeout with no retries. Passes in 1s (was 60s timeout).

title: HTT: Cost Management API returns 0 rows despite Cost Management Reader role
description:
## Summary
Cost sync for HTT-CORE subscription (32a28177-6fb2-4668-a528-6d6cafb9665e) returns 0 rows from Cost Management query API, even though:
- `Cost Management Reader` RBAC role is assigned ✅
- `az consumption usage list` returns usage data (PostgreSQL, etc.)
- API returns HTTP 200 with valid columns but empty `rows: []`

## Root Cause (Likely)
The subscription is likely under a **CSP (Cloud Solution Provider)** or **MCA (Microsoft Customer Agreement)** billing model. In these models, cost data is exposed at the billing account/profile level, NOT via the subscription-scoped Cost Management query endpoint.

## Options
1. Query at billing account scope instead of subscription scope
2. Use the Consumption Usage Details API (`az consumption usage list`) which does return data
3. Check billing model: `az billing account list` to confirm CSP/MCA

## Affected
- HTT-CORE subscription: 32a28177-6fb2-4668-a528-6d6cafb9665e
- Other 4 tenants have 0 subscriptions (Entra-only)

title: CRITICAL: .env.production template committed to git with placeholder secrets

title: TLL & DCE: Entra ID Premium P1 license required for signInActivity + MFA reports
description:
## Summary
TLL (Lash Lounge) and DCE (Delta Crown) return `Authentication_RequestFromNonPremiumTenantOrB2CTenant` for:
- `signInActivity` on /users endpoint (requires AuditLog.Read.All + **Entra ID P1**)
- `/reports/authenticationMethods/userRegistrationDetails` (requires Reports.Read.All + **Entra ID P1**)

## Current State
- Admin consent ✅ — both permissions are in the token (confirmed via JWT decode)
- API returns 403 with `Authentication_RequestFromNonPremiumTenantOrB2CTenant`
- Our code gracefully degrades: users/guests/admins/SPs all sync, just without signInActivity and MFA data

## Action Required
Purchase Entra ID P1 licenses for TLL and DCE tenants in Microsoft 365 Admin Center, OR accept the graceful degradation as-is.

## Affected Tenants
- TLL: 3c7d2bf3-b597-4766-b5cb-2b489c2904d6
- DCE: ce62e17d-2feb-4e67-a115-8ea4af68da30

title: Fix auth mocking in route + integration tests (67 xfail/xpass)
description:
## Summary
65 tests across 7 files fail with 401 Unauthorized because auth is not properly mocked.
67 tests in the same files pass (xpass). All are marked `xfail(strict=False)`.

## Affected Files
- tests/unit/test_routes_dmarc.py (9 fail, 2 pass)
- tests/unit/test_routes_identity.py (10 fail, 8 pass)
- tests/unit/test_routes_resources.py (9 fail, 6 pass)
- tests/unit/test_routes_costs.py (7 fail, 5 pass)
- tests/unit/test_routes_compliance.py (8 fail, 5 pass)
- tests/integration/test_tenant_isolation.py (8 fail, 13 pass)
- tests/integration/test_riverside_api.py (14 fail, 12 pass)

## Root Cause
These tests hit endpoints requiring authentication but don't provide a valid auth mock.
The passing tests either test auth-required behavior (expecting 401) or test endpoints
that don't require auth.

## Fix
Add a shared auth fixture that provides a mock JWT token/session for authenticated route tests.
